summary: Frontend-only verification of the P0 redirect-loop regression fix in CreatorHub. All 8 flows from the review request passed via Playwright browser automation against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. No redirect loop observed in any scenario. Token + zustand persist state are now kept perfectly consistent by clearAuth()/hardLogout() in api.js and the bootstrap useEffect in App.js.

verified_scenarios.scenario: Owner jane@creatorhub.io/password123 login lands on /dashboard, tokens + zustand populated, sidebar + dashboard render
Hard reload on /dashboard (page.reload + goto) stays on /dashboard; 0 navigations to /login
Access token corrupted ('garbage.invalid.token'), refresh token valid -> axios interceptor silently refreshed exactly once, access token length grew to 268, URL stayed on /dashboard

Both access AND refresh corrupted -> hardLogout() triggered: access/refresh localStorage cleared, zustand persist key 'ch-auth-v1' entirely removed, redirected to /login with only 2 /login navigations (no infinite loop)
marcus@creatorhub.io (must_change_password=true) login routes to /change-password; direct navigation to /dashboard is blocked and stays on /change-password
tom@creatorhub.io (scheduler) after password change sees EXACTLY [Posts, Tasks] in sidebar (nav-posts, nav-tasks). No Connections, no extras. Matches review spec exactly.
Logout button (data-testid=logout-button) clears ch_access_token, ch_refresh_token, sets zustand user=null, navigates to /login. Post-logout /dashboard attempt redirects back to /login.

Concurrent 401s via multiple parallel API calls after corrupting access token on /analytics -> isRefreshing + queue pattern produced EXACTLY 1 call to /api/auth/refresh, user stayed authenticated, no redirect loop

critical_code_review_comments: api.js hardLogout() uses window.location.replace('/login') which is a full page reload. Works (actually helps zustand re-hydrate from empty storage) but is heavier than a React-Router navigate. Fine for this edge case.

store.js logout() calls clearAuth() and sets user=null, but the zustand persist middleware then writes back the cleared state as a serialized object with user:null (observed in T7: zustand key still present with null user). This is functionally correct (RedirectIfAuthed only checks user truthiness) but means 'clear' is asymmetric between hardLogout (removes key entirely via localStorage.removeItem) and explicit logout (rewrites key with null user). Consider aligning both paths — call localStorage.removeItem('ch-auth-v1') after set({user:null,...}) in logout() for true parity.

App.js bootstrap useEffect: when user exists but no access token, it calls logout() WITHOUT navigating. Current behaviour works because RedirectIfAuthed on /login lets you through once user=null, and protected routes bounce to /login via ProtectedRoute. No-op risk is low; just documenting.

Iteration 2 reported Connections visible in Tom/Priya sidebars (conflict with review spec). In iteration 3, Tom's sidebar correctly shows ONLY Posts + Tasks — so this appears to have been tightened since iter2. Priya was NOT re-verified in iter3 to preserve her seeded credential state, but the same filter should apply.

Test credentials note: seed_data is idempotent (only seeds if users collection empty), so Tom's password from iter2 (newpass123) persists across backend restarts. In iter3 the login succeeded with temp1234, indicating the DB was fresh OR seed was re-run — worth confirming seed_data() behaviour on startup.
test_credentials: Jane=password123 (unchanged). Marcus=temp1234 (unchanged — login attempt in T5 does NOT change password, it only reaches /change-password screen). Tom=newpass123 (CHANGED in T6 during this iteration — was temp1234 before). Priya=unchanged from prior iteration. Main agent should re-seed DB before running T6 again.
seed_data_creation: No test data created. Only Tom's password was mutated as part of T6 (newpass123).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The P0 redirect-loop fix is fully verified end-to-end. Key regression tests to preserve going forward: (a) setting ch_access_token='garbage' + valid refresh must silently refresh and stay on /dashboard; (b) setting both tokens to garbage must wipe ch-auth-v1 entirely and land on /login exactly once; (c) logout button must clear both localStorage tokens; (d) App.js bootstrap must handle user-without-token by logging out. All these now pass. Tom's password was mutated to newpass123 during T6 — re-seed backend if re-running.
rca_of_the_issue: Original root cause (per problem statement): auth state was split across two storage keys. localStorage 'ch_access_token'/'ch_refresh_token' were owned by the axios interceptor, while zustand persisted 'ch-auth-v1' with the user object. When refresh failed, the old interceptor cleared only the two token keys. RedirectIfAuthed read `user` from zustand (still populated) and bounced the user back into the app, which immediately fired an authenticated request with no token -> 401 -> refresh fails -> loop. The fix introduces a single clearAuth() helper that removes BOTH token keys AND the zustand persist key, called from hardLogout() in the interceptor and from store.logout(). Additionally App.js adds a bootstrap useEffect that, on every app mount, reconciles: if zustand has a user but localStorage has no access token (ghost session), it calls logout() before rendering routes. This closes the window where a stale zustand user could trigger protected routes before the first API call. Verified in iter3 that T4 (both-corrupted) cleanly lands on /login with only 2 navigations (initial direct goto + redirect), no infinite loop, and zustand key entirely wiped.
